# Feature: Run Reporting â€” Foundation

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-002
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_run_reporting.md`

Build the platform-agnostic foundation that all three run-report builders (FEAT-003 / FEAT-004 / FEAT-005) compose. This feature ships a new pure-TypeScript module `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/` that provides:

- Types describing the inputs builders accept (metadata, summary, request detail).
- A single trusted HTML/attribute escape pair (the security contract for every builder).
- Format helpers (timestamp, duration, filename sanitization) and content helpers (text-vs-binary detection).
- A theme CSS string and an interactivity JS string that are inlined into every produced report.
- A reusable `renderShell`, `renderHeader`, `renderSummary`, and â€” the centerpiece â€” a `renderRequestDetail(RunRequestData)` block that produces the expand/collapse + Request/Response/Validation tabs used identically across all three report types.

There is no I/O here. Every function accepts fully-resolved inputs and returns a string. The module has zero React, zero DOM, zero Node dependencies â€” it can be bundled into both the VS Code webview and the web app.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature defines new TypeScript types for the report-builder inputs only â€” none are persisted.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`ReportMetadata`** (new type in `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/types.ts`)
  - `runType` (`'collection' | 'flow' | 'testsuite'`) â€” drives header copy and filename.
  - `subjectName` (`string`) â€” collection / flow / suite display name.
  - `itemPath` (`string[] | undefined`) â€” only for collection runs scoped to a folder; rendered as `parent / sub / leaf`.
  - `environmentName` (`string | undefined`) â€” selected default environment, or `undefined` when "None" was chosen.
  - `defaultAuthName` (`string | undefined`) â€” selected default auth profile name (option (a) from the plan: name only, never the credential value).
  - `concurrentCalls` (`number | undefined`) â€” collection-runner setting; `undefined` for flow/testsuite.
  - `delayBetweenCalls` (`number | undefined`) â€” collection-runner setting; `undefined` for flow/testsuite.
  - `startedAt` (`number`) â€” epoch ms.
  - `completedAt` (`number | undefined`) â€” epoch ms; undefined if the run was cancelled mid-flight.
  - `totalElapsedMs` (`number | undefined`) â€” derived; included for resilience even when one of the timestamps is missing.

- **`ReportSummary`** (new type)
  - `total` (`number`), `passed` (`number`), `failed` (`number`), `skipped` (`number`), `averageTimeMs` (`number | undefined`).

- **`ReportRequestNode`** (new type â€” the input to `renderRequestDetail`)
  - Mirrors the public fields of [RunRequestData](packages/core/src/components/common/RunRequestCard.tsx) (`id`, `name`, `method`, `url`, `folderPath`, `runStatus`, `responseStatus`, `responseTime`, `validationStatus`, `validationResult`, `responseHeaders`, `responseBody`, `isResponseEncoded`, `error`).
  - Plus the originating `request: AnyCollectionRequest` so request headers / params / body can be rendered in the Request tab.
  - Reuses (not duplicates) `RunStatus`, `ValidationStatus`, and `ValidationResult` from existing core modules.

### Relationships

- N/A.

### Indexes & Constraints

- N/A.

### Behavioral Constraints

- All escape helpers MUST be the single trusted pair from `escape.ts`. No builder is allowed to assemble HTML by raw template-literal interpolation of dynamic data.
- Theme CSS and interactivity JS are **static string constants**. They never interpolate dynamic data â€” DOM coupling is achieved exclusively via pre-escaped `data-*` attributes and indexed `<template>`/`<pre>` blocks.

---

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts` â€” Public exports.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/types.ts` â€” `ReportMetadata`, `ReportSummary`, `ReportRequestNode`, plus shared union aliases.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/escape.ts` â€” `escapeHtml(input: string): string`, `escapeAttr(input: string): string`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/format.ts` â€” `formatTimestamp(epochMs)`, `formatDuration(ms)`, `sanitizeFilename(name)`, `buildReportFilename(runType, subjectName, now)`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/content.ts` â€” `isTextResponse(headers, body, isEncoded)`, `prettyPrintIfJson(body, contentType)`, `BINARY_PLACEHOLDER` constant.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/theme.ts` â€” `THEME_CSS` (single string).
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/interactivity.ts` â€” `INTERACTIVITY_JS` (single string for expand/collapse + tab switching, no dependencies).
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/shell.ts` â€” `renderShell({ title, body }) â†’ string` â€” wraps theme + interactivity + body in a complete `<!DOCTYPE html>` document.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/blocks/header.ts` â€” `renderHeader(metadata: ReportMetadata) â†’ string`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/blocks/summary.ts` â€” `renderSummary(summary: ReportSummary) â†’ string`.
- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/blocks/requestDetail.ts` â€” `renderRequestDetail(node: ReportRequestNode) â†’ string`.
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/reporting/escape.test.ts`

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** â€” not strictly required (this feature ships no UI), but nothing meaningful flows from this feature without `exportFile` to write the produced strings. Recommend FEAT-001 lands first or in parallel.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Define types and the trusted escape pair

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `types.ts` with `ReportMetadata`, `ReportSummary`, `ReportRequestNode`, and the runtype union. Create `escape.ts` exporting `escapeHtml` and `escapeAttr` â€” `escapeHtml` replaces `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'` (in that order, `&` first); `escapeAttr` does the same and additionally encodes any backtick. Both must handle empty / `undefined` (`undefined` â†’ `''`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `escape.test.ts` â€” exhaustive table tests covering each special character individually, mixed payloads, repeated `&`, surrogate pair safety, empty string, `undefined` input. Negative tests: feed `<script>alert(1)</script>` and assert the literal escaped form is returned with no `<` / `>` characters remaining.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on every exported type and function (purpose, inputs, return, security note for the escape pair).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/escape` passes.

### TASK-002: Format and content helpers

- [ ] **Implementation**: `format.ts` exports `formatTimestamp(epochMs)` returning `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` local time, `formatDuration(ms)` returning a human-readable string (`"1.23s"`, `"42ms"`, `"2m 14s"`), `sanitizeFilename(name)` collapsing non-`[A-Za-z0-9_-]` to `-` and trimming leading/trailing `-`, and `buildReportFilename(runType, subjectName, now)` producing `wave-{runType}-{sanitizedName}-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.html`. `content.ts` exports `isTextResponse(headers, body, isEncoded)` returning `false` if `isEncoded === true`, if `Content-Type` matches `image/*|audio/*|video/*|application/octet-stream|application/pdf|application/zip`, or if the first 64 bytes of `body` contain non-printable characters; otherwise `true`. `prettyPrintIfJson(body, contentType)` returns `JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(body), null, 2)` when the content type contains `json` and the body parses; otherwise the original body. Export `BINARY_PLACEHOLDER` as `'(binary content not included)'`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `format.test.ts` covers each formatter with happy / edge inputs (timestamp boundary, duration `0`/`999`/`1000`/`60_000`, sanitize with unicode/whitespace/path separators, filename composition). `content.test.ts` table-tests `isTextResponse` over: explicit text content types, JSON, octet-stream, common image types, missing/empty content type, `isEncoded: true` short-circuit, raw body sniff with non-printable bytes. Tests for `prettyPrintIfJson` with valid JSON, malformed JSON (returns original), non-JSON content type (returns original).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on each exported function â€” be explicit about local vs UTC for `formatTimestamp`.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/format reporting/content` passes.

### TASK-003: Theme CSS and interactivity JS string constants

- [ ] **Implementation**: `theme.ts` exports `THEME_CSS` â€” a self-contained CSS string covering: page reset, typography, summary tile grid, status pills (success / failed / skipped / running / pending / idle), method badges (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE), the request-detail card, the tab strip, the `pre` blocks for headers / body / validation, dark-friendly default palette. No `@import`, no external fonts. `interactivity.ts` exports `INTERACTIVITY_JS` â€” a plain `function-scoped IIFE` (no top-level globals) that: (a) wires click handlers to elements with `data-toggle="card"` to toggle a sibling element with the matching `data-card-body`, (b) wires click handlers to `data-tab="<tab>"` buttons to set the `aria-selected` / `hidden` state of sibling tab panels with matching `data-tab-panel`. The script never reads DOM text content or interpolates user data â€” it only flips classes / attributes. Both constants are pure strings exported as `export const THEME_CSS = '...'` and `export const INTERACTIVITY_JS = '...'`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: For each constant, assert (1) the export is a non-empty string, (2) does not contain `<` or `>` outside of CSS/JS contexts (sanity check that no HTML leaked into the string), (3) `INTERACTIVITY_JS` does not reference `window.<anything>` at the top level (sanity scan), (4) loading both into a jsdom `Document` (theme via `<style>`, JS via `<script>`) and triggering a synthetic click on a `data-toggle="card"` element flips the linked `data-card-body` from hidden to visible.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc explaining that both constants are static and must never interpolate dynamic data â€” reference the security contract from the plan.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/theme reporting/interactivity` passes (or whichever test file is created â€” the JSDom integration may live in `shell.test.ts` instead).

### TASK-004: Shell, header, and summary blocks

- [ ] **Implementation**: `shell.ts` exports `renderShell({ title, body })` returning a complete document: `<!DOCTYPE html>`, `<html lang="en">`, `<head>` with `<meta charset="utf-8">`, `<title>{escapeHtml(title)}</title>`, `<style>{THEME_CSS}</style>`, then `<body>{body}<script>{INTERACTIVITY_JS}</script></body></html>`. `body` is trusted (it is composed by other reporting blocks that already escape). `title` is escaped here. `blocks/header.ts` exports `renderHeader(metadata)` rendering the wave-client wordmark, run type label, subject name + optional `itemPath`, and a metadata strip with environment, default auth, concurrency, delay (omitted when undefined), started/completed timestamps, total elapsed. `blocks/summary.ts` exports `renderSummary(summary)` with five tiles (Total / Passed / Failed / Skipped / Avg Time); the avg-time tile renders `-` when `averageTimeMs` is `undefined`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `shell.test.ts` â€” assert structural anchors: doctype present, exactly one `<style>` and one `<script>`, the script appears before the closing `</body>`, the `THEME_CSS` and `INTERACTIVITY_JS` constants are present in the output. `header.test.ts` â€” table tests over different metadata combinations (env present/absent, auth present/absent, concurrency/delay both omitted for flow/testsuite). XSS regression: feed `<script>alert(1)</script>` into `subjectName` and assert no `<script>` substring appears in the produced HTML. `summary.test.ts` â€” covers all five tiles, including the `-` rendering for missing avg time.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc explaining that `renderShell.body` is treated as already-escaped and that callers must produce only escaped HTML.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/shell reporting/blocks/header reporting/blocks/summary` passes.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003.

### TASK-005: Reusable request detail block (Request / Response / Validation tabs)

- [ ] **Implementation**: `blocks/requestDetail.ts` exports `renderRequestDetail(node: ReportRequestNode)`. The block renders the same shape as [RunRequestCard.tsx](packages/core/src/components/common/RunRequestCard.tsx) but as static HTML: a card header with method badge, name, URL, status indicator, elapsed time; a body (hidden by default via `data-toggle`/`data-card-body`) containing a tab strip with three `data-tab` buttons (Request / Response / Validation) and three matching `data-tab-panel` panels. The Request panel renders headers + params + body of the originating request (use `isHttpRequest` / `isWsRequest` type guards from existing core utils to handle protocol differences gracefully â€” WS/SSE in this report context render whatever is available without the body / method fields that don't apply). The Response panel renders status, elapsed, headers, and body â€” body is wrapped through `prettyPrintIfJson` then `escapeHtml`; if `isTextResponse` returns `false`, render `BINARY_PLACEHOLDER` instead. The Validation panel renders the per-rule list from `validationResult` (rule name, expected, actual, pass/fail). When `error` is present, surface it at the top of the Response panel in red. Every dynamic value goes through `escapeHtml` / `escapeAttr` â€” no exceptions.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `blocks/requestDetail.test.ts` covers (1) HTTP request happy path: assert presence of method badge text, URL, response status, response body. (2) Failed request: error message is rendered, response panel shows error block. (3) Skipped status: card shows "Skipped" indicator. (4) Binary response: response body region contains `BINARY_PLACEHOLDER` and not the raw body. (5) JSON response: pretty-printed body present with two-space indent. (6) WS request input: no body / no method-specific markup; runs without throwing. (7) **XSS regression battery**: feed `<script>alert(1)</script>` into name, URL, request header value, response header value, response body, validation message â€” assert produced HTML contains the literal escaped form (`&lt;script&gt;`) and zero un-escaped `<script>` substrings. (8) `isResponseEncoded === true` â†’ binary placeholder.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `renderRequestDetail` describing the block's output structure, the binary-skip behavior, and the cross-protocol handling.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/blocks/requestDetail` passes. Spot-check one produced HTML output by writing it to a temp file and opening in a browser to confirm rendering + click behavior (record the smoke result in the verify notes).
- **Depends on**: TASK-004.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: The report header surfaces only the *name* of the chosen default auth profile (option (a) from the plan). Credential values are never accessed by this module.
- **Error Handling**: This module is pure. Every function returns a string and never throws on user input â€” malformed inputs (e.g., un-parseable JSON) fall back to safe defaults (raw body, `''`, `'-'`). Callers can assume no exceptions.
- **Logging**: N/A â€” no I/O.
- **Security**: The escape pair in `escape.ts` is the single trusted boundary. Any new builder introduced later in FEAT-003 / FEAT-004 / FEAT-005 must route every dynamic value through it. Reviewers should treat raw template-literal interpolation of dynamic data as a defect.

---
